verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1) is a member of the CRISP/CAP superfamily expressed predominantly in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen, where it associates with the sperm surface. It lacks its own transmembrane domain or GPI anchor but binds tightly to the sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet in a wash-resistant, non-covalent manner, facilitating sperm-egg fusion. This expression and surface display is restricted to a narrow reproductive/germline-associated tissue context (epididymis-secreted, sperm-surface-bound). This qualifies as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes, given the narrow reproductive lineage restriction.
